perturbation.original_text: PageMaster Corporation in conjunction with Go Call, shall offer free new Motorola "Wordline Alphanumeric" (or equal) pagers with no activation fee to all customers responding to this promotion who purchase twelve (12) months of numeric paging and airtime products and services from PageMaster Corporation ("Purchase Customers").
perturbation.changed_text: PageMaster Corporation in conjunction with Go Call, shall offer free new Motorola "Wordline Alphanumeric" (or equal) pagers with no activation fee to all consumers responding to this promotion who purchase twelve (12) months of numeric paging and airtime products and services from PageMaster Corporation ("Purchase Customers").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'customers' has been changed to 'consumers.' While seemingly similar, 'customers' implies a pre-existing business relationship, while 'consumers' suggests anyone responding to the promotion, regardless of prior interaction. This creates uncertainty about who qualifies as a 'Purchase Customer' later in the agreement, leading to potential disputes regarding fulfillment obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Purchase Customer will be required to purchase twelve months of local numeric airtime at a rate of $10.33 per month through a designated nationwide airtime provider, prepaid in advance. The purchased airtime shall be non-refundable to the consumer. Additionally, Purchase Customers will be required to pay for shipping and handling costs and applicable sales taxes based on their locations.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'customers' to 'consumers' broadens the scope of who can be a 'Purchase Customer'. The original text implies a pre-existing relationship, while the changed text includes anyone responding to the promotion. The contradicted text states 'Each Purchase Customer will be required to purchase twelve months of local numeric airtime at a rate of $10.33 per month through a designated nationwide airtime provider, prepaid in advance. The purchased airtime shall be non-refundable to the consumer'. The use of 'consumer' here makes it ambiguous whether the non-refundability applies to all consumers who respond to the promotion or only those who were pre-existing customers and who became 'Purchase Customers'. This ambiguity makes it difficult to enforce the terms, as it's unclear who qualifies as a 'Purchase Customer' obligated to the airtime purchase, leading to an in-text contradiction as described in Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: PageMaster Corporation will contract with a nationwide airtime service provider to fulfill and to ship Purchasing Customer orders direct to the Purchase Customers to fulfill this promotion in a timely manner. PageMaster Corporation has chosen for the purpose of this promotion, MetroCall Inc. to provide pager and airtime services where the nationwide airtime service provider has the facilities and the requisite governmental authority to provide such services. All Purchase Customers shall become customers of the nationwide airtime service provider. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be allowed to market additional pagers arid enhanced services to all Purchase Customers, and to charge for over-calls with respect to any account with a Purchase Customer. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be able to discontinue or terminate service to any Purchase Customer in accordance with the terms of the contract between the nationwide airtime service provider and the Purchase Customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, PageMaster Corporation shall remain solely responsible for the fulfillment of all services and obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: PageMaster Corporation will contract with a nationwide airtime service provider to fulfill and to ship Purchasing Customer orders direct to the Purchase Customers to fulfill this promotion in a timely manner. PageMaster Corporation has chosen for the purpose of this promotion, MetroCall Inc. to provide pager and airtime services where the nationwide airtime service provider has the facilities and the requisite governmental authority to provide such services. All Purchase Customers shall become clientele of the nationwide airtime service provider. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be allowed to market additional pagers arid enhanced services to all Purchase Customers, and to charge for over-calls with respect to any account with a Purchase Customer. The nationwide airtime service provider shall be able to discontinue or terminate service to any Purchase Customer in accordance with the terms of the contract between the nationwide airtime service provider and the Purchase Customer. Notwithstanding the foregoing, PageMaster Corporation shall remain solely responsible for the fulfillment of all services and obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'customers' has been changed to 'clientele'. This introduces a different connotation, implying a more exclusive or managed relationship. This could lead to confusion regarding the rights and responsibilities of the airtime service provider towards those who obtain pagers through the promotion, particularly regarding marketing and service termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, PageMaster Corporation shall remain solely responsible for the fulfillment of all services and obligations set forth in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'customers' to 'clientele' is a semantic difference but doesn't create a strong in-text contradiction with the clause stating PageMaster remains solely responsible for services and obligations. While 'clientele' might imply a closer relationship, it doesn't necessarily negate PageMaster's overarching responsibility. Both statements can coexist without causing a direct conflict regarding who is ultimately responsible for fulfilling the agreement's terms. The change is subtle and open to interpretation, and therefore doesn't meet the threshold for a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Go Call agrees not to use or advertise any trademarks, logos or other property rights of PageMaster Corporation or any third parties participating in the promotion. Any advertising, artwork, presentation, or other promotional activities (collectively "Advertising") concerning the pager Promotion not pre-approved in writing by PageMaster Corporation shall be deemed to be unauthorized by PageMaster Corporation and shall constitute a breach of this Agreement. In addition to the duty to indemnify PageMaster Corporation as provided in Paragraph 9 hereof, Go Call shall also have the duty to indemnify Motorola, Inc. or any affiliated entity from and against any and all claims, expense, suits or demands arising from such unauthorized Advertising by Go Call, or its agent, affiliate, licensee, franchisee or any other third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon termination or expiration of this Agreement, Go Call agrees not to use or advertise any trademarks, logos or other property rights of PageMaster Corporation or any third parties participating in the campaign. Any advertising, artwork, presentation, or other promotional activities (collectively "Advertising") concerning the pager Promotion not pre-approved in writing by PageMaster Corporation shall be deemed to be unauthorized by PageMaster Corporation and shall constitute a breach of this Agreement. In addition to the duty to indemnify PageMaster Corporation as provided in Paragraph 9 hereof, Go Call shall also have the duty to indemnify Motorola, Inc. or any affiliated entity from and against any and all claims, expense, suits or demands arising from such unauthorized Advertising by Go Call, or its agent, affiliate, licensee, franchisee or any other third party.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'promotion' has been changed to 'campaign'. This subtle change, while seemingly innocuous, creates ambiguity. 'Promotion' is the defined term for the specific activities under this agreement, whereas 'campaign' is broader and undefined. This could lead to disputes as to whether Go Call's actions after the agreement, even if related to the pager service, fall under the restrictions related to 'unauthorized Advertising.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any advertising, artwork, presentation, or other promotional activities (collectively "Advertising") concerning the pager Promotion not pre-approved in writing by PageMaster Corporation shall be deemed to be unauthorized by PageMaster Corporation and shall constitute a breach of this Agreement. In addition to the duty to indemnify PageMaster Corporation as provided in Paragraph 9 hereof, Go Call shall also have the duty to indemnify Motorola, Inc. or any affiliated entity from and against any and all claims, expense, suits or demands arising from such unauthorized Advertising by Go Call, or its agent, affiliate, licensee, franchisee or any other third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'promotion,' a defined term within the contract. The changed text replaces 'promotion' with 'campaign,' an undefined term. The contradicted text uses the defined term 'Promotion' to describe activities covered by the agreement. By changing 'promotion' to the undefined 'campaign,' the changed text introduces ambiguity as to what activities are now covered, thus contradicting the section that relies on the defined term 'Promotion.' This ambiguity creates a direct conflict, as it is unclear if the indemnity clause still applies to activities related to the pager service if they are considered part of a broader 'campaign' rather than the specified 'Promotion.' Therefore, it is a strong in-text contradiction based on misaligned terminology.
